rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Seven And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's motion is denied. Responding party objected to the requests for production by stating that she had previously produced all documents related to the relevant trust and that she would not produce other documents because they contained private financial information. There is no reason to order production of documents to which petitioner already has access through other productions. The requests were each dramatically overbroad, and petitioner has failed to show that documents which have not been produced are either relevant or reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of relevant information. Exploring a statement that may have been made in the course of mediation cannot be a basis for finding relevance. Even if petitioner had demonstrated relevance, the requested documents include privileged personal financial interests and tax records as to which petitioner has not shown a sufficient countervailing interest to overcome the privilege. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, May 14, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IRMA JEAN GARRETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Attorney requests extraordinary compensation in the amount of $40,036.50. 2) Attorney requests continuing extraordinary fees and estimated costs of litigation of up to $60,000 to defend against the civil suit filed by Kenneth Garrett. 3) Attorney and Petitioners request full statutory fees, but administration has not been completed. They allege that all preliminary work has been completed, and what remains is resolution of the property dispute and final distribution. 4) Petitioners request waiver of bond, and if not, reduction to value as of the date of death ($155,000 on 10/17/95). Fair market value is reported to be $854,847 as of 3/20/19. 5) Petitioners request administration be allowed to continue for 1 year, due to pending litigation.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Orders
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/20/19 - An appearance will be required on that date. However, examiner notes that there may be a notice issue. The caption does not specify the relief requested, as required by CRC 7.102. The notice served includes some, but not all of the relief. If it is determined that there are additional parties who are entitled to notice, such as additional beneficiaries to the Trust, Eva's heirs-at-law, or Peter's current wife, the Petition will need to be amended to correct the caption and 30 days' notice will be required to all interested parties. Examiner has not reviewed the objections or points & authorities of Peter as no courtesy copies were received. Endorsed-filed copies of all documents must be received in Room 202 by 5/14/19 to ensure review prior to the 5/20/19 hearing.

rulings.Rulings: continued to june 26, 2019 (first available date) for proper notice. the notice of hearing did not include the mandatory language in Probate Code section 851(c) and gives notice to one beneficiary c/o another person. in addition, the petition does not identify the heirs of the Trustor, and heirs are entitled to notice. a statement that the Trustor did not have children and parents and grandparents are unknown is not sufficient.
